title: [Bug]: Oracle payload_field idempotency accepts empty path segments
summary: Oracle payload-field idempotency configuration accepts dotted paths with empty segments.
observed: A payload_field value with an empty path segment validates but cannot resolve to a clear payload key.
expected: payload_field configuration must reject empty path segments and control characters during validation.
reproduction: Run the focused regression test that configures payload_field with an empty dotted path segment.
impact: Misconfigured idempotency can be discovered only during message processing rather than during validation.
delivery_semantics: The fix fails invalid Oracle sink configuration before messages are fetched or ACKed.
security: Clear allow-list validation reduces ambiguous idempotency-key extraction.
acceptance: Oracle payload_field rejects empty path segments.
Valid dotted payload fields continue to work.
The focused regression test and full checks pass.
tests: Run the focused regression test, Oracle mapping tests, Oracle sink contract tests, and the full local check script.
documentation: Update Oracle idempotency documentation or release notes if payload_field validation is clarified.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the fix is published with regression and verification evidence.
